Precaution_1: Discontinue suspected causative drug immediately under medical supervision
Precaution_2: Seek emergency care if symptoms include rash, swelling, or breathing difficulty
Precaution_3: Report the reaction to a doctor and document the drug name for future reference
Precaution_4: Do not restart the drug without explicit physician approval

Precaution_1: Start antimalarial prophylaxis (atovaquone-proguanil, doxycycline, or mefloquine) as prescribed before, during, and after travel to endemic areas
Precaution_2: Use repellents (DEET ≥20%), permethrin-treated clothing, and sleep under bed nets in malaria-endemic regions
Precaution_3: Seek immediate medical attention and malaria blood test if fever develops within 3 months of returning from endemic areas
Precaution_4: Complete the full prescribed treatment course (including primaquine for P. vivax/P. ovale to prevent relapse) and rest adequately during illness

Precaution_1: Identify and strictly avoid known allergens (food, environmental, or contact)
Precaution_2: Carry prescribed epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) if at risk for anaphylaxis
Precaution_3: Consult an allergist for skin prick testing and consider allergen immunotherapy
Precaution_4: Wear a medical alert bracelet identifying severe allergies

Precaution_1: Take levothyroxine daily at the same time, on an empty stomach (30–60 minutes before breakfast), as instructed without missing doses
Precaution_2: Avoid soy-based foods, high-fiber supplements, calcium, iron, and multivitamins within 4 hours of levothyroxine dose, as these impair absorption
Precaution_3: Monitor TSH and free T4 levels every 6–12 months or after dose adjustments; report symptoms of over-replacement (palpitations, weight loss, insomnia)
Precaution_4: Eat a balanced diet with adequate iodine (iodized salt); avoid excessive cruciferous vegetables and goitrogenic foods in large amounts

Precaution_1: Moisturize skin daily with thick emollients immediately after bathing to reduce dryness
Precaution_2: Avoid known triggers: stress, smoking, excessive alcohol, skin trauma, and certain medications (beta-blockers, lithium)
Precaution_3: Use prescribed topical corticosteroids or vitamin D analogues as directed
Precaution_4: Protect affected skin from sunburn while using controlled UV light therapy if prescribed

Precaution_1: Avoid trigger foods: fatty/fried foods, chocolate, caffeine, alcohol, tomatoes, and spicy foods
Precaution_2: Elevate head of bed by 15–20 cm and avoid lying down within 3 hours after meals
Precaution_3: Maintain healthy body weight and quit smoking to reduce LES pressure
Precaution_4: Take proton pump inhibitors (PPIs) or H2 blockers as prescribed; avoid long-term unsupervised PPI use

Precaution_1: Take fat-soluble vitamin supplements (A, D, E, K) to compensate for malabsorption
Precaution_2: Follow a low-fat, high-complex-carbohydrate diet to reduce bile load
Precaution_3: Use prescribed antipruritic agents (cholestyramine, rifampicin) to relieve itching
Precaution_4: Undergo regular monitoring of liver function tests and bone density

Precaution_1: Maintain strict hand hygiene, especially after toilet use and before handling food
Precaution_2: Avoid raw shellfish, untreated water, and raw produce in regions with poor sanitation
Precaution_3: Get vaccinated against hepatitis A, particularly before international travel to endemic areas
Precaution_4: Rest adequately and avoid alcohol and hepatotoxic medications during recovery

Precaution_1: Engage in low-impact exercise (swimming, cycling, walking) to maintain joint mobility and strengthen periarticular muscles
Precaution_2: Achieve and maintain healthy body weight to reduce mechanical joint loading
Precaution_3: Use assistive devices (knee braces, orthotics, walking aids) to unload affected joints
Precaution_4: Apply heat/cold therapy for pain relief; use paracetamol or topical NSAIDs as first-line pharmacological treatment

Precaution_1: Engage in low-impact exercise (swimming, cycling, walking) to maintain joint mobility and strengthen periarticular muscles
Precaution_2: Achieve and maintain healthy body weight to reduce mechanical joint loading
Precaution_3: Use assistive devices (knee braces, orthotics, walking aids) to unload affected joints
Precaution_4: Apply heat/cold therapy for pain relief; use paracetamol or topical NSAIDs as first-line pharmacological treatment

Precaution_1: Perform prescribed canalith repositioning maneuver (Epley maneuver) as instructed by physician
Precaution_2: Avoid rapid head movements; change head position slowly when lying down or getting up
Precaution_3: Remove fall hazards from the home environment given risk of imbalance during attacks
Precaution_4: Avoid activities requiring balance (driving, working at heights) until episodes are controlled

Precaution_1: Always carry fast-acting glucose (glucose tablets, fruit juice, regular soda) to treat acute episodes
Precaution_2: Monitor blood glucose regularly, especially before driving or engaging in physical activity
Precaution_3: Do not skip meals; consume regular balanced meals and snacks if on insulin or sulfonylureas
Precaution_4: Educate family members on glucagon injection administration for severe hypoglycemia emergencies

Precaution_1: Cleanse face twice daily with a gentle, non-comedogenic, pH-balanced cleanser; avoid scrubbing
Precaution_2: Use non-comedogenic, oil-free moisturizers and sunscreens to prevent pore blockage
Precaution_3: Apply prescribed retinoids, benzoyl peroxide, or topical antibiotics consistently as directed
Precaution_4: Avoid picking or squeezing lesions, which worsens inflammation and increases scarring risk

Precaution_1: Adhere strictly to a balanced diet low in refined carbohydrates and saturated fats
Precaution_2: Exercise regularly (at least 150 minutes of moderate activity per week) to improve insulin sensitivity
Precaution_3: Monitor blood glucose levels daily and HbA1c every 3 months as advised by physician
Precaution_4: Take prescribed medications or insulin on schedule and attend regular diabetic foot and eye examinations

Precaution_1: Keep affected areas clean; wash gently with mild soap and water and apply topical antibiotic as prescribed
Precaution_2: Cover sores with gauze bandages to prevent spread and discourage scratching
Precaution_3: Avoid skin-to-skin contact with others until sores have healed; wash clothing and bedding in hot water
Precaution_4: Complete the full course of prescribed oral or topical antibiotics even if symptoms improve early

Precaution_1: Adopt the DASH diet (rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, low-fat dairy, low sodium <2g/day)
Precaution_2: Exercise aerobically for at least 150 minutes per week; reduce sedentary time
Precaution_3: Avoid smoking, limit alcohol intake, and practice stress management techniques (mindfulness, yoga)
Precaution_4: Monitor blood pressure at home regularly and take antihypertensive medications exactly as prescribed

Precaution_1: Avoid NSAIDs and aspirin; use paracetamol for pain relief when possible
Precaution_2: Complete the full H. pylori eradication regimen (triple or quadruple therapy) as prescribed
Precaution_3: Eliminate alcohol, quit smoking, and reduce caffeine intake to allow mucosal healing
Precaution_4: Eat smaller, more frequent meals; avoid eating 2–3 hours before bedtime

Precaution_1: Increase dietary fiber intake (25–35g/day from fruits, vegetables, whole grains) and fluid intake to soften stools
Precaution_2: Avoid straining during defecation and prolonged sitting on the toilet
Precaution_3: Use sitz baths (warm water soaks for 15 minutes, 2–3 times daily) to relieve pain and inflammation
Precaution_4: Apply prescribed topical anesthetics or corticosteroid ointments; consult a colorectal surgeon if symptoms persist

Precaution_1: Rest adequately and maintain good hydration with warm fluids (broths, herbal teas, water)
Precaution_2: Practice frequent hand washing with soap for at least 20 seconds and avoid touching face
Precaution_3: Use saline nasal irrigation or steam inhalation to relieve nasal congestion
Precaution_4: Avoid antibiotics (ineffective against viruses); use paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever and pain relief

Precaution_1: Maintain strict isolation from non-immune individuals (pregnant women, immunocompromised) until all lesions have crusted
Precaution_2: Trim fingernails short and use mittens in young children to minimize scratching and prevent bacterial superinfection
Precaution_3: Apply calamine lotion, colloidal oatmeal baths, and use antihistamines for pruritus relief; never use aspirin (Reye's syndrome risk)
Precaution_4: Administer antiviral therapy (acyclovir) within 24 hours of rash onset for high-risk patients; vaccinate susceptible contacts promptly

Precaution_1: Maintain good posture with ergonomic neck support during work; avoid prolonged forward neck flexion (phone/computer use)
Precaution_2: Perform prescribed cervical strengthening and stretching exercises daily under physiotherapy guidance
Precaution_3: Use a cervical pillow or firm mattress to maintain neutral neck alignment during sleep
Precaution_4: Seek urgent medical review if neurological symptoms develop (arm weakness, hand numbness, gait problems, or bladder/bowel dysfunction)

Precaution_1: Take prescribed antithyroid medications (methimazole, carbimazole, or PTU) consistently at scheduled times
Precaution_2: Avoid iodine-rich foods (seaweed, kelp supplements, iodine-containing contrast agents) and iodine-containing medications
Precaution_3: Protect eyes from sunlight, dust, and wind with wraparound sunglasses if eye disease (Graves' orbitopathy) is present
Precaution_4: Monitor thyroid function tests regularly; avoid caffeine and stimulants that worsen palpitations and anxiety

Precaution_1: Drink at least 2–3 liters of water daily to flush bacteria from the urinary tract
Precaution_2: Urinate promptly after sexual intercourse; avoid holding urine for extended periods
Precaution_3: Complete the full prescribed antibiotic course even if symptoms resolve after a few days
Precaution_4: Women should wipe from front to back after using the toilet; avoid harsh soaps, feminine sprays, and douches in the genital area

Precaution_1: Wear graduated medical compression stockings (20–30 mmHg or as prescribed) during prolonged standing or sitting
Precaution_2: Elevate legs above heart level for 15–30 minutes several times daily to improve venous return
Precaution_3: Engage in regular walking, cycling, or swimming to activate the calf muscle pump
Precaution_4: Avoid prolonged standing, crossing legs, and tight-fitting clothing around the waist or thighs

Precaution_1: Adhere strictly to prescribed antiretroviral therapy (ART) to achieve and maintain undetectable viral load
Precaution_2: Practice safer sex (consistent condom use) to prevent HIV transmission and re-infection with drug-resistant strains
Precaution_3: Avoid sharing needles, syringes, or other drug injection equipment; use needle exchange programs
Precaution_4: Attend regular follow-up appointments for CD4 count, viral load monitoring, and prophylaxis against opportunistic infections

Precaution_1: Control blood pressure strictly within target range to prevent re-bleeding or recurrent stroke
Precaution_2: Engage in intensive inpatient and outpatient rehabilitation (physiotherapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy) as early as possible
Precaution_3: Maintain a safe home environment with grab bars, ramps, and fall prevention modifications
Precaution_4: Adhere to all prescribed medications (antihypertensives, anticoagulants if indicated) and attend regular neurological follow-up

Precaution_1: Drink only boiled or bottled water and avoid raw fruits and vegetables washed with untreated water in endemic areas
Precaution_2: Practice strict hand hygiene before meals and after toilet use; ensure food is cooked thoroughly
Precaution_3: Complete the full prescribed antibiotic course (fluoroquinolone or cephalosporin) without interruption to prevent relapse
Precaution_4: Get vaccinated against typhoid before traveling to endemic regions; vaccinate household contacts of confirmed cases

Precaution_1: Ensure all close contacts and household members are vaccinated with the complete HBV vaccine series
Precaution_2: Practice safe sex with consistent condom use; avoid sharing personal items (razors, toothbrushes, nail clippers) that may be contaminated with blood
Precaution_3: Avoid alcohol and over-the-counter hepatotoxic medications; consult physician before taking any new supplement or drug
Precaution_4: Attend regular liver function monitoring and hepatocellular carcinoma screening (ultrasound + AFP every 6 months) if chronically infected

Precaution_1: Keep skin clean, dry, and well-ventilated; change socks and underwear daily; dry between toes thoroughly
Precaution_2: Avoid sharing towels, shoes, combs, or nail equipment; use shower sandals in public locker rooms and pools
Precaution_3: Apply prescribed antifungal creams, powders, or oral antifungals consistently for the recommended duration; do not stop early
Precaution_4: Manage underlying conditions (diabetes, immunosuppression) that predispose to fungal infections; maintain good glycemic control

Precaution_1: Avoid sharing needles, syringes, or drug preparation equipment; access harm reduction and needle exchange programs
Precaution_2: Do not share personal hygiene items (razors, nail clippers, toothbrushes) that may be blood-contaminated
Precaution_3: Consult a hepatologist regarding direct-acting antiviral (DAA) therapy for a potentially curative 8–12 week course
Precaution_4: Avoid alcohol entirely to slow liver fibrosis progression; screen household and sexual contacts for HCV testing

Precaution_1: Maintain a consistent daily schedule for sleep, meals, hydration, and exercise; erratic routines are common migraine triggers
Precaution_2: Keep a detailed headache diary to identify food triggers (tyramine-rich foods, alcohol, MSG, caffeine withdrawal), environmental triggers, and hormonal patterns
Precaution_3: Take prescribed acute therapy (triptan, NSAID, or combination) at the earliest sign of an attack before pain becomes severe
Precaution_4: Discuss prophylactic therapies (topiramate, propranolol, amitriptyline, CGRP monoclonal antibodies, onabotulinum toxin A) with neurologist if migraines occur >4 days per month or are significantly disabling

Precaution_1: Identify and minimize exposure to known allergens and irritants (dust mites, pollen, pet dander, smoke, strong odors)
Precaution_2: Use prescribed preventer inhaler (inhaled corticosteroid) daily even when symptom-free; never stop without medical advice
Precaution_3: Always carry reliever bronchodilator (salbutamol) inhaler and have a written asthma action plan for exacerbations
Precaution_4: Check peak flow regularly; seek emergency care immediately if severe breathlessness, blue lips, or reliever provides no relief

Precaution_1: Stop all alcohol consumption immediately and permanently; even small amounts worsen liver damage and mortality
Precaution_2: Follow a high-calorie, high-protein nutritional plan with vitamin supplementation (thiamine, folate, multivitamins) under dietitian guidance
Precaution_3: Take prescribed medications (corticosteroids for severe cases, pentoxifylline) exactly as directed
Precaution_4: Attend regular hepatologist appointments for liver function monitoring and liver fibrosis assessment; consider addiction counseling and support groups

Precaution_1: Seek immediate medical evaluation to identify the underlying cause (liver disease, hemolysis, bile duct obstruction)
Precaution_2: Maintain adequate hydration and rest; avoid alcohol and hepatotoxic drugs until liver function normalizes
Precaution_3: Follow a low-fat diet to reduce bile salt load if cholestatic jaundice is present
Precaution_4: Attend follow-up tests (LFTs, bilirubin, ultrasound, ERCP if needed) promptly as directed by physician

Precaution_1: Drink only boiled or purified water and avoid raw/undercooked pork and deer meat in regions where HEV genotype 3/4 is endemic
Precaution_2: Practice rigorous hand hygiene, especially in areas with inadequate sanitation and flooding
Precaution_3: Pregnant women should avoid travel to HEV-endemic regions and seek immediate medical care if symptoms of hepatitis develop
Precaution_4: Immunocompromised patients should discuss HEV vaccination (rHEV-239, licensed in China) and ribavirin treatment with their specialist

Precaution_1: Use insect repellent containing DEET, picaridin, or IR3535 and wear long protective clothing during daylight hours when Aedes mosquitoes are most active
Precaution_2: Eliminate all standing water (tyres, flower pots, buckets, drains) near home to destroy mosquito breeding sites
Precaution_3: Monitor platelet count and hematocrit daily during fever; seek hospital admission if dengue warning signs appear (abdominal pain, vomiting, bleeding, rapid breathing, pallor)
Precaution_4: Avoid aspirin, ibuprofen, and NSAIDs, which increase bleeding risk; use only paracetamol for fever and pain

Precaution_1: Vaccinate against Hepatitis B, which simultaneously prevents HDV infection (no HBV = no HDV)
Precaution_2: Avoid sharing needles, syringes, drug paraphernalia, and blood-contaminated personal items
Precaution_3: Undergo regular liver function tests and HBV/HDV viral load monitoring if co-infected
Precaution_4: Discuss antiviral treatment (pegylated interferon-alpha or bulevirtide) with a hepatologist for chronic HDV infection

Precaution_1: Call emergency services (ambulance) immediately at the first sign of chest pain, pressure, or related symptoms; do not drive yourself
Precaution_2: Chew 300mg aspirin (unless truly allergic) immediately while awaiting emergency services if a heart attack is suspected
Precaution_3: Do not eat, drink, or exert yourself; rest in a comfortable position (typically semi-recumbent) until help arrives
Precaution_4: After hospitalization, adhere strictly to prescribed secondary prevention medications (aspirin, statins, beta-blockers, ACE inhibitors) and enroll in cardiac rehabilitation

Precaution_1: Get annual influenza vaccine and pneumococcal vaccine (PCV15/PCV20 for adults >65 and high-risk groups) to prevent the most common precipitating infection
Precaution_2: Avoid smoking and exposure to air pollutants that impair mucociliary clearance and lung defense mechanisms
Precaution_3: Practice good hand hygiene; ensure adequate nutrition and vitamin D levels to support immune function
Precaution_4: In hospitalized patients, implement aspiration precautions, early mobilization, and incentive spirometry to prevent hospital-acquired pneumonia

Precaution_1: Engage in regular low-impact exercise (swimming, cycling, tai chi) to maintain joint function and muscle strength; avoid high-impact activities during flares
Precaution_2: Maintain healthy body weight to reduce biomechanical stress on joints
Precaution_3: Take prescribed disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (DMARDs, biologics) consistently for inflammatory arthritis; do not self-discontinue
Precaution_4: Apply hot packs for stiffness and cold packs for acute inflammation; use assistive devices and seek occupational therapy for joint protection

Precaution_1: Rehydrate promptly using ORS; infants should continue breastfeeding; avoid plain water alone as it does not replace electrolytes
Precaution_2: Introduce a normal, age-appropriate diet as early as tolerated; prolonged dietary restriction delays recovery
Precaution_3: Report cases of suspected food poisoning to public health authorities to identify outbreak sources
Precaution_4: Cook all meat, poultry, eggs, and shellfish to safe temperatures; refrigerate perishables promptly; avoid cross-contamination between raw and cooked foods

Precaution_1: Complete the full HRZE treatment regimen for the recommended duration (minimum 6 months); never self-discontinue even when feeling better
Precaution_2: Notify the local public health authority to enable contact tracing; all close contacts should receive TB skin test or IGRA and treatment for latent TB if indicated
Precaution_3: Ensure home is well-ventilated with natural airflow; use N95 respirators and separate living quarters until confirmed non-infectious (3 consecutive negative sputum AFB smears)
Precaution_4: Eat a nutritious, high-protein diet to support immune function and recovery; address alcohol use, smoking, and diabetes which impair anti-TB immunity

Precaution_1: Isolate immediately if symptomatic or test-positive; wear a well-fitted N95/FFP2 mask around others and in healthcare settings
Precaution_2: Stay up to date with COVID-19 vaccinations and boosters, particularly for high-risk individuals (elderly, immunocompromised, comorbidities)
Precaution_3: Monitor oxygen saturation with a pulse oximeter; seek immediate emergency care if SpO2 falls below 94%, or severe breathlessness, chest pain, or confusion develop
Precaution_4: Rest, stay well hydrated, and manage symptoms with paracetamol; take prescribed antivirals (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir, remdesivir) early if eligible to reduce severe disease risk

Precaution_1: Follow a low glycemic index diet rich in fiber, vegetables, lean protein, and whole grains; limit refined carbohydrates, sugary drinks, and saturated fats
Precaution_2: Exercise at least 150 minutes per week (brisk walking, cycling, swimming) to improve insulin sensitivity and glycemic control
Precaution_3: Monitor blood glucose as directed; maintain HbA1c <7% (or individualized target); take prescribed medications (metformin, GLP-1 agonists, SGLT2 inhibitors) as directed
Precaution_4: Attend regular screening for diabetic complications: annual eye examination, urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio, foot examination, and lipid and blood pressure monitoring

Precaution_1: Use prescribed daily controller inhaler (ICS or ICS/LABA) consistently even when well; poor adherence is the leading cause of uncontrolled asthma
Precaution_2: Minimize exposure to known triggers: use HEPA air purifiers, encase mattress/pillows in allergen-proof covers, maintain humidity below 50%
Precaution_3: Follow a written asthma action plan; carry a reliever inhaler at all times and use a spacer device for better drug delivery
Precaution_4: Undergo annual spirometry to assess lung function and review inhaler technique with healthcare provider at every visit

Precaution_1: Consume iron-rich foods (red meat, legumes, fortified cereals, tofu, leafy greens) paired with vitamin C-rich foods to enhance non-heme iron absorption
Precaution_2: Avoid tea, coffee, and calcium-rich foods immediately before and after iron-rich meals as they inhibit iron absorption
Precaution_3: Take prescribed iron, B12, or folate supplements as directed; IV iron infusion may be needed for severe deficiency or poor absorption
Precaution_4: Identify and treat the underlying cause of anemia (e.g., control menorrhagia, treat gastric ulcer, eradicate H. pylori, treat hemolytic disease) to prevent recurrence

Precaution_1: Seek emergency medical care immediately for severe, persistent, or worsening right lower abdominal pain, especially with fever and nausea
Precaution_2: Do not eat or drink anything if appendicitis is suspected (to prepare for possible surgery)
Precaution_3: Avoid taking laxatives or applying heating pads to the abdomen, as this may mask symptoms or worsen perforation risk
Precaution_4: Follow all post-operative wound care instructions after appendectomy; return to hospital immediately if signs of wound infection, persistent fever, or increasing pain develop

Precaution_1: Control blood pressure to target <130/80 mmHg with prescribed antihypertensives (ACE inhibitors or ARBs are preferred for their renoprotective effects)
Precaution_2: Follow a kidney-friendly diet: restrict sodium, potassium, phosphorus, and protein (under dietitian supervision) as eGFR declines
Precaution_3: Maintain optimal glucose control if diabetic; use SGLT2 inhibitors (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin) as they slow CKD progression
Precaution_4: Avoid nephrotoxic agents (NSAIDs, aminoglycoside antibiotics, iodinated contrast without adequate precaution); stay well hydrated; attend regular nephrology follow-up

Precaution_1: Begin oral rehydration immediately using ORS or clear fluids; avoid solid foods until vomiting subsides, then reintroduce bland foods gradually
Precaution_2: Do not take anti-diarrheal agents if bloody diarrhea is present or botulism is suspected; seek urgent medical review
Precaution_3: Practice safe food handling: cook foods to recommended temperatures, refrigerate leftovers promptly, avoid cross-contamination between raw and cooked foods
Precaution_4: Report suspected food poisoning (especially from commercial establishments) to local health authorities; seek emergency care if botulism symptoms develop (double vision, drooping eyelids, difficulty swallowing, muscle weakness)
